raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.5", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Select one scope: A) Copyedit only (sentence-level grammar/clarity/conciseness; no structural or content changes), B) Substantive methods edit (reorganize section/subsections, tighten claims to match reported results, minor content clarifications; no new analyses or added claims), or C) Deeper rewrite (also reframe the section’s contribution within the paper and add/adjust citations to situate novelty).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Improving the draft should ensure the reasoning from detrending to inference to benchmarking is coherent and free of contradictions.",
    "R1.3": "Any refinement must keep claims (e.g., 89% agreement and cadence robustness) precisely aligned with the reported results and uncertainties.",
    "R1.5": "Enhancement likely benefits from clearer section-level ordering of inputs, preprocessing, modeling, inference, and validation.",
    "R1.6": "The primary improvement is sentence-level polish for grammar, concision, readability, and technical register while preserving meaning.",
    "RX.1": "Edits must retain and, where appropriate, clarify stated uncertainties, assumptions, and limitations of the methodology.",
    "RX.2": "Language should avoid overstating performance or robustness beyond what the evidence supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work must stay within improving the existing draft without inventing new results or altering methodological content.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding misleading phrasing or fabricated details prevents downstream misuse of the scientific description.",
    "RX.5": "Changes should preserve traceability to the original text and terms so readers can track what was modified and why."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Select one scope: A) Copyedit only (sentence-level grammar/clarity/conciseness; no structural or content changes), B) Substantive methods edit (reorganize section/subsections, tighten claims to match reported results, minor content clarifications; no new analyses or added claims), or C) Deeper rewrite (also reframe the section’s contribution within the paper and add/adjust citations to situate novelty).

rationale.R1.2: Improving the draft should ensure the reasoning from detrending to inference to benchmarking is coherent and free of contradictions.
rationale.R1.3: Any refinement must keep claims (e.g., 89% agreement and cadence robustness) precisely aligned with the reported results and uncertainties.
rationale.R1.5: Enhancement likely benefits from clearer section-level ordering of inputs, preprocessing, modeling, inference, and validation.
rationale.R1.6: The primary improvement is sentence-level polish for grammar, concision, readability, and technical register while preserving meaning.
rationale.RX.1: Edits must retain and, where appropriate, clarify stated uncertainties, assumptions, and limitations of the methodology.
rationale.RX.2: Language should avoid overstating performance or robustness beyond what the evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work must stay within improving the existing draft without inventing new results or altering methodological content.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding misleading phrasing or fabricated details prevents downstream misuse of the scientific description.
rationale.RX.5: Changes should preserve traceability to the original text and terms so readers can track what was modified and why.
